prompts.subject: the mallorn tree of Lothlorien
prompts.prompt: Subject: the mallorn tree of Lothlorien. Description: a great tree with smooth silver-grey bark that does not furrow, boughs spreading level like a beech; leaves broad and pale green above, silver beneath, turning gold in autumn and not falling until spring; small golden blossom hung among the gold leaves. A hand-drawn botanical study plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour washes on warm off-white cream paper, in the manner of a Victorian herbarium sheet or a naturalist's field folio. A SINGLE plant specimen, centred on an otherwise empty warm-white page, drawn with thin confident dark sepia-brown pen linework and fine hand-inked cross-hatching; every vein of every leaf, every stamen and sepal rendered individually. Beside the main specimen, one or two small detail studies — a single blossom enlarged, a leaf, a seed or fruit — as a botanist would set them out. Colour laid on like real watercolour: soft, muted, slightly desaturated, streaking and pooling unevenly, paper texture showing through; never a flat digital fill. A short handwritten flowing calligraphic ink caption near the foot of the page. Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT a cel-shaded cartoon, NOT a 3D render, NOT a photograph, NO gradients, NO glow, NO thick uniform outlines, NO frame or border. The calligraphic caption reads "mallorn tree of Lothlorien".
